_comment: chrome.storage.local fixture for testing the PromptMate rating prompt banner. This data lives in Extension Storage (chrome.storage.local), NOT in the page's localStorage. Do not use localStorage.setItem — it has no effect on extension storage.
storage_location: chrome.storage.local — visible in Chrome DevTools → Application tab → Extension Storage → Local

seen_no_action._description: Banner was shown at least once (firstSeenAt set) but user has not rated or dismissed. Inject this to reliably surface the 'A QUICK FAVOR' banner on next panel open.

eligible_unseen._description: Banner has never been shown. Extension will show it once usage threshold is met. Less predictable for testing — prefer seen_no_action.

dismissed._description: User clicked X on the banner. Banner should not reappear immediately.

rated._description: User clicked Rate PromptMate. Banner should not reappear.

method_application_tab._description: Preferred method for agents — pure UI, no console switching required.
steps: Open Chrome DevTools on the supported platform page (F12 or right-click → Inspect)
In the left sidebar expand 'Extension Storage' then 'Local'
Find the PromptMate extension entry and click it
Double-click the value cell and replace it with the desired state JSON from this file
Open PromptMate panel — the banner should appear at the top

method_service_worker_console._description: Alternative — requires navigating to chrome://extensions first.
steps: Find PromptMate and click 'service worker' (or 'background page') to open its DevTools
In that console run the snippet below replacing VALUE with the desired state JSON
Navigate back to the platform page and open PromptMate panel
